top_10.rationale: SimCLR (Chen et al., 2020) — the canonical contrastive self-supervised learning framework. The primary source for understanding contrastive representation learning.

Lil'Log 'Self-Supervised Representation Learning' — Lilian Weng's authoritative overview covering contrastive methods, pretext tasks, and the state of self-supervised learning. One of the most widely cited tutorials on the topic.
arXiv survey 'A Survey on Contrastive Self-supervised Learning' — comprehensive survey covering pseudo labels, self-supervised learning frameworks, and downstream task applications.

arXiv 'Supervised Contrastive Learning' (Khosla et al.) — important extension of contrastive learning to supervised settings, consistently outperforming cross-entropy; key paper in the contrastive learning literature.
ScienceDirect 'A comprehensive survey on contrastive learning' — Elsevier journal publication with broad coverage of contrastive self-supervised representation learning methods.
MDPI Technologies 'A Survey on Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning' — open-access survey covering pseudolabels, self-defined supervision, and contrastive objectives.
GraphCL (You et al.) — contrastive self-supervised learning for graph representations. Key extension of contrastive SSL to graph-structured data.
'Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning' by Ankesh Anand — well-written blog post explaining how contrastive SSL techniques build representations by learning to encode what makes two things similar.
ACM DL 'Supervised contrastive learning' — NeurIPS proceedings paper on supervised contrastive learning; formal publication venue for the key extension to supervised setting.

Springer 'Contrastive Self-supervised Representation Learning Using Synthetic Data' — peer-reviewed article on contrastive SSL with synthetic data augmentation, covering discriminative representation learning.
